title: KeywordPlan
description: PRE-collection ad-search keyword plan (수요 기반 키워드 확장). An LLM expands ONE product/persona into a broad, multi-angle keyword set across three axes — 핵심 니즈(Needs) / 사용 맥락(Use-case) / 연관 카테고리(Adjacency) — and flattens them into keyword queries that feed Meta keyword search (run-flow.mjs --from-keyword-plan). Goal is COVERAGE (volume), not precision: same-product relevance does not matter because hooks transfer across products; quality/fit is the human's later cut. NOT the post-analysis keyword-model.schema.json (which has corpus/tf-df stats and functional ad-slots) — this is a fresh, cold, intent-axis artifact.

target_market.description: language/market scope the queries are written for, e.g. KR

axes.description: the three expansion axes; each is a list of terms with a one-line rationale (used for the user-facing announce + provenance)

needs.description: 핵심 니즈 — the underlying job/desire the buyer is solving

use_case.description: 사용 맥락 — when/where the behavior happens (occasion/scene)

adjacency.description: 연관 카테고리 — products the same buyer also seeks (concrete co-sought nouns)

queries.description: flattened search queries (the collection input). Every query traces to an axis.

query.description: the actual search string for Meta keyword search

mode.description: keyword-plan queries are always keyword mode; competitor/advertiser queries come from the competitor track

rationale.description: one line: why this term belongs to this axis
